claim: Agentic coding orchestration: Wrap Aider as an MCP server inside Claude Code to delegate expensive GPT-4o output token costs to cheaper models (Gemini 2.5 Pro, Llama 4)
actionable: Create MCP wrappers around cheaper coding tools; use Claude Code as orchestrator calling Aider with your choice of LLM backend

claim: Llama 4 Behemoth and Maverick models are beating ChatGPT-4 and Gemini 2.5 Pro on benchmarks; open-source models now compete with SOTA closed-source

claim: Claude Code's primary cost is $15/million output tokens; delegating AI coding to cheaper models (e.g., Gemini 2.5 Pro at ~$5/million) reduces costs 3x while maintaining quality

claim: MCP tool return values matter most: return diffs and summaries, not raw output; this minimizes tokens consumed by the orchestrator
